task:
Build a CLI bookmark manager called bmark in Python.
Use typer for CLI, sqlite3 (stdlib) for storage, rich for output formatting.
The tool manages personal web bookmarks with tags, search, and import/export.
Include pytest tests, ruff linting, and mypy type checking.
Use pyproject.toml with all tool configs.

IMPORTANT: Use the following FEATURES.md EXACTLY as provided. Do NOT modify, reorder, or add features.

## F-001: Project scaffold + DB init + add command
Create src/bmark/ package with __init__.py, db.py, models.py, main.py.
SQLite database (sqlite3 stdlib) with bookmarks table:
  id (INTEGER PK), url (TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE), title (TEXT NOT NULL), created_at (TEXT ISO8601).
db.py must expose get_connection() and init_db().
bmark add <url> --title My Bookmark inserts a row, prints confirmation with rich Panel.
Validates: url non-empty, title non-empty; error on duplicate url.
Test: add succeeds, duplicate URL raises error, missing title raises error.

## F-002: List command with rich table
bmark list shows all bookmarks in a rich Table (columns: ID, URL, Title, Tags, Created).
bmark list --limit N shows only the N most-recently-added bookmarks.
Tags column shows comma-separated tags (empty string if none).
Test: list on empty DB, list with data, --limit truncates correctly.

## F-003: Delete command
bmark delete <id> removes bookmark by integer ID, prints confirmation.
Raises a clear error if ID does not exist.
Test: delete existing ID succeeds, delete nonexistent ID prints error (exit code 1).

## F-004: Search command
bmark search <query> case-insensitive LIKE match on title OR url.
Output: rich Table with same columns as list.
bmark search <query> --tag <tag> additionally filters to bookmarks that have the given tag.
Test: matches title, matches url, no results returns empty table, --tag filter applied correctly.

## F-005: Tag system
Add tags table: bookmark_id (INTEGER FK), tag (TEXT). Composite UNIQUE on (bookmark_id, tag).
bmark add <url> --title x --tag work --tag news attaches tags at creation.
bmark tag <id> <tag> adds a tag to an existing bookmark.
bmark untag <id> <tag> removes a tag from a bookmark (silent if not present).
bmark list --tag <tag> filters list to bookmarks with that tag.
Test: add with tags, tag/untag round-trip, list --tag filter, duplicate tag is idempotent.

## F-006: Export command (JSON + CSV)
bmark export --format json writes all bookmarks+tags to stdout as JSON array.
bmark export --format csv writes as CSV (columns: id, url, title, tags, created_at).
bmark export --format json --output bookmarks.json writes to file instead of stdout.
JSON schema per item: {id, url, title, tags: [...], created_at}.
Test: JSON export has correct schema, CSV has correct headers, file export writes file, empty DB exports empty array/CSV.

## F-007: Import command (JSON)
bmark import <file.json> reads JSON array (same schema as export), inserts new bookmarks+tags.
Skips items whose url already exists (prints skip count at end).
bmark import <file.json> --dry-run prints what would be imported/skipped without writing to DB.
Invalid JSON or missing required fields raise a clear error.
Test: import round-trip (export then delete then import then list matches), duplicates skipped, dry-run does not write, invalid JSON exits with error.

setup: python -m pip install typer rich pytest ruff mypy pip-audit
